qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL Playground exposes master key in client-side HTML without authentication
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes credential exposure via the GraphQL Playground embedding the master key in client-side HTML, while the advisory describes stored XSS via SVG file upload. These are different vulnerability classes, affected components, and root causes.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: LDAP injection in authentication adapter allows authorization bypass via unsanitized user input
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes LDAP injection in the authentication adapter, while the advisory describes stored XSS via SVG file upload. These are different vulnerability classes, affected components, and attack vectors.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: LiveQuery regex evaluation uses unsafe fallback without timeout, enabling event-loop-blocking ReDoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a ReDoS vulnerability in LiveQuery regex evaluation, while the advisory describes stored XSS via SVG file upload. These are different vulnerability classes, affected components, and root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-hcj7-6gxh-24ww, CVE-2026-30948) describes a stored XSS vulnerability via SVG file upload in Parse Server where authenticated users can upload SVG files containing JavaScript that execute in the browser. The fix adds 'svg' to the file extension denylist. None of the three qualifying findings describe this root cause: (1) the graphql-playground finding describes master key exposure in GraphQL Playground HTML, (2) the ldap-injection finding describes LDAP injection in authentication adapter, and (3) the livequery-redos finding describes ReDoS in LiveQuery regex evaluation. All three findings address completely different vulnerability classes, affected components, and root causes.
